questions.title: Testlet 3

The athletic trainer assesses the injury as a chest contusion and permits the athlete to return to the game late in the fourth quarter. The athletic trainer tells the football player that if his condition gets worse, he is to call the athletic trainer or go to the emergency room. However, after going home, the football player reports to his mother that his chest continues to hurt. His mother tells him to “tough it out” and try to get some sleep. The athlete continues to have pain in his left rib cage, and in the middle of the night, his left shoulder starts to hurt. The athlete decides to wait until morning to tell his mother. When he doesn’t respond to his mother’s call to breakfast, she goes into his room and finds him unresponsive and in shock. The mother calls for an ambulance, and the player is then rushed to the hospital.
